title: QA: hook errors silently discarded by fire_post_tool; OWASP A09 logging failure
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/hooks/mod.rs:347-369, 420-447
## Violation
`fire_post_tool` is the convenience wrapper used by every tool execution
call site. It runs `self.run(event, &input).await` and discards the
result:
```rust
let _ = self.run(event, &input).await;
```
The `HookResult` discarded contains `errors: Vec<HookError>` populated in
`run` at lines 441-444 (`hook_result.errors.push(e)`). Audit-trail,
security-monitoring, and compliance hooks that fail (timeout, command
failed, parse error) leave no signal to the caller, only a
`tracing::error!` that may not be ingested. Violates OWASP A09 (Security
Logging and Monitoring Failures). Tool execution must never silently
skip its audit trail.
## Mandated refactor
Return `Result<(), Vec<HookError>>` from `fire_post_tool` and force every
call site to handle the error case (fail the tool call when an audit
hook fails). At minimum, emit a structured-log entry per discarded error
including session_id, tool_name, hook command, exit code, and increment
a counter operators can scrape. Stop using `let _ =` to silence the
result.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
